title: Core DAO - Open App
meta_description: core dao is the official route to Core Mainnet resources, staking, dApps, and the explorer so you can open the right app fast.
hero_h2: Bitcoin-aligned L1 access for BTCfi and EVM apps
hero_lead: core dao is the official route to a Bitcoin-aligned, EVM-compatible layer-one network for BTCfi apps, staking access, bridge routes, and mainnet activity.
what_is: Core is a Bitcoin-aligned, EVM-compatible layer-one blockchain built around Satoshi Plus consensus, BTC staking, CORE staking, and a growing BTCfi ecosystem.

how.d: Start from the official Core domain and avoid sponsored look-alike links before connecting a wallet.
Use the official Core Mainnet details: Chain ID 1116, CORE as the currency symbol, and Core Scan as the explorer.
Browse dApps, staking, bridge access, and developer resources from the official Core navigation.
Review wallet prompts, gas token requirements, and transaction status before approving any action.

points.p: Satoshi Plus connects Bitcoin miner participation, CORE staking, and BTC staking with an EVM-compatible execution layer.
Core Mainnet is documented with Chain ID 1116, CORE as the currency symbol, official RPC, explorer, and staking site in the network configuration.
Use the official explorer to check addresses, blocks, token contracts, and transaction status before trusting copied links.

faq.a: core dao refers to Core, a Bitcoin-aligned, EVM-compatible layer-one blockchain for BTCfi, staking, and smart-contract applications.

faq.q: Is core dao compatible with Ethereum wallets?
faq.a: Yes. Core Mainnet is EVM-compatible, and the official docs list Chain ID 1116, CORE as the currency symbol, RPC details, and explorer links.

faq.q: Where can I explore core dao transactions?
faq.a: Use Core Scan, the official explorer for blocks, transactions, addresses, and token contracts on Core Mainnet.

faq.q: Does core dao support Bitcoin staking?
faq.a: Core supports self-custodial Bitcoin staking through its official staking route, with BTC timelocking used as part of network participation.

faq.q: What token pays gas on core dao?
faq.a: CORE is the native currency symbol used for Core Mainnet transaction fees and network activity.

footer_note: This page is not the official app; verify coredao.org before connecting a wallet. Educational only, not financial advice.
